headline: Chips Crater as Hormuz Fears Lift Crude; Breadth Turns Decisively Negative
body_html:
Tuesday's session ended with sellers firmly in control across the broader market, as 242 names in our 395-stock universe finished lower against just 148 advancers. The dominant theme was unmistakable: geopolitical tension in the Strait of Hormuz drove a sharp wedge through the tape, hammering cyclical and technology names while sending energy traders scrambling for exposure. Reports of Iran attacking tankers along a key U.S. Navy route, combined with a raised threat level at the strait, injected a risk-off tone that persisted through the close.

Sector rotation played out in stark relief. Semiconductors bore the worst of the selling, sliding 5.55% as a group, with test-equipment and analog names leading declines. Materials fell 2.53% and Industrials dropped 2.50%, the latter punctuated by Middleby's staggering 22.3% plunge — the session's single most dramatic move. Against that backdrop, Energy advanced 0.51% and Healthcare edged up 0.44%, the only sectors to close meaningfully in the green. Chord Energy gained 4.7% to pace the winners' list, with EOG Resources adding nearly 4%.

Middleby's collapse aside, the session's quieter story was the resilience of internet-services and research-subscription names — GoDaddy, Gartner, and CoStar each gained more than 4%. When hardware crumbles and software-adjacent names hold, the market is sending a message worth watching into Wednesday.

briefs.headline: Middleby Suffers Historic Single-Day Drop
briefs.body: Industrial equipment maker Middleby tumbled 22.3% on Tuesday, the sharpest decline in our universe and the session's defining individual-stock event. The move dragged the broader Industrials sector down 2.5%, amplifying an already difficult day for cyclical names. No other stock came close to matching the magnitude of the selloff.

briefs.headline: Strait of Hormuz Threat Level Elevated to 'Severe'
briefs.body: Authorities raised the Hormuz threat designation to its highest tier after Iran was blamed for striking tankers operating along a route used by the U.S. Navy. Qatar formally accused Tehran of responsibility for at least one of the attacks. The escalation rattled shipping and energy markets globally, with crude-linked equities among the few beneficiaries in Tuesday's session.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Sector Takes Its Steepest Decline of the Year
briefs.body: The chip complex shed 5.55% as a group on Tuesday, with Teradyne, Alpha and Omega Semiconductor, Semtech, and Allegro MicroSystems each falling between 8.9% and 9.6%. The selloff was broad-based across power, analog, and test-equipment sub-segments, underscoring how vulnerable the group remains to any shift in risk appetite.

briefs.headline: Energy Names Buck the Tape on Geopolitical Tailwind
briefs.body: With the Hormuz situation front and center, Energy was the session's top-performing sector, adding 0.51% while nearly everything else retreated. Chord Energy led all gainers in our universe with a 4.7% advance, and EOG Resources was not far behind at roughly 4%. Saudi Arabia's decision to trim crude prices appeared insufficient to offset supply-disruption fears.

briefs.headline: Amazon's Landmark Bond Sale Draws Market Attention
briefs.body: A $25 billion bond offering from the e-commerce and cloud giant drew scrutiny Tuesday as analysts parsed what the sizable debt issuance signals about capital-allocation priorities and balance-sheet strategy. Separately, Microsoft's shifting approach to artificial intelligence model development was a talking point among technology investors navigating a difficult tape.

briefs.headline: Khamenei Funeral Processions Draw Global Attention Amid Regional Tensions
briefs.body: The coffin of Iran's slain supreme leader arrived in Iraq's Najaf on Tuesday, according to state television reports, as vast crowds gathered to mourn across the region. The developments added a layer of uncertainty to an already fraught geopolitical backdrop, with Israel's prime minister separately telling a U.S. news outlet that he and President Trump share broad alignment on Iran policy.
